content.problemStatement:
Chef's phone has a total storage of SSS MB. Also, Chef has 222 apps already installed on his phone which occupy XXX MB and YYY MB respectively.

He wants to install another app on his phone whose memory requirement is ZZZ MB. For this, he might have to delete the apps already installed on his phone. Determine the minimum number of apps he has to delete from his phone so that he has enough memory to install the third app.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains four integers S,X,YS, X, YS,X,Y and ZZZ — the total memory of Chef's phone, the memory occupied by the two already installed apps and the memory required by the third app.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of apps Chef has to delete from his phone so that he can install the third app.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: The unused memory in the phone is 777 MB. Therefore Chef can install the 333 MB app without deleting any app.
Test Case 2: There is no unused memory in the phone. Chef has to first delete one of the apps from the phone and then only he can install the 111 MB app.
Test Case 3: There is no unused memory in the phone. Chef has to first delete both the apps from the phone and then only he can install the 151515 MB app.
Test Case 4: The unused memory in the phone is 505050 MB. Chef has to first delete the 303030 MB app from the phone and then only he can install the 757575 MB app.
